packet_label: ThirdPerson human inputs on 2026-04-19 local time
explicit_request: Reduce human intervention burden by enforcing the ThirdPerson human default-lane regression contract, using valid evidence surfaces, answering direct questions directly, and honoring stop/go gates.

needs.label: Trustworthy default-lane proof
needs.description: When 'regression' is claimed, proof must exercise the repo's human default lane and use valid in-runtime evidence.

needs.label: Hard stop/go semantics
needs.description: STOP/PAUSE and approval gates must be treated as hard execution state; no tools run past the stop without a resume.

needs.label: Direct answers in the requested shape
needs.description: For agree/disagree and yes/no questions, answer first and explicitly before continuing work.

needs.label: Low-friction approval surfaces
needs.description: When asking for plan approval, provide a diff and concrete links so the human can review without reconstructing state.

needs.label: Root-cause debugging discipline
needs.description: Follow first-disagreement tracing to root cause rather than repeated symptom-level iteration.

inferred: high cognitive load due to proof-surface mismatch and repeated re-asking
suffering_assessment.notes: INFERRED elements are conservative and anchored to repeated boundary resets and evidence disputes.

evidence.excerpt: Task failed my basic regression test... invisible pawn.
Regression must exercise the human default lane... Agree/disagree?
STOP. I asked you a question.
I don't have a diff for plan.md... how am i supposed to approve.
Evidence invalid; re-take screenshot with feet in full view.
Determine root cause... follow the disagreement upstream.

local_constraints_applied.note: Treat REGRESSION.md as canonical for lane definitions and regression closure criteria.

assumptions: INFERRED: lane-proof burden is currently the most central burden, but it coexists with stop/go, answer-shape, approval, and debugging burdens rather than replacing them.
OBSERVED: the packet contains repeated explicit stop/go and approval gating instructions.

recommended_next_step.summary: Implement a default-lane regression proof gate (lane id + artifact contract) and a STOP/PAUSE protocol that prevents tool work past explicit stops. Make approval requests include diffs and links by default.

self_sufficiency_improvements.improvement: Answer direct questions first (yes/no or agree/disagree in line 1).
Never claim regression without naming the lane and attaching a lane-appropriate artifact.
When requesting approval, include diff + links + one explicit approval question.

values_check.truthfulness: Explicitly records missing transcripts and avoids claiming tests were run.
values_check.compassion: Treats interventions as signals of burden and aims to reduce burden without blaming the human.
values_check.tolerance: Keeps multiple orthogonal solution options and sequences winners instead of forcing a single bet.

how_could_the_system_have_inferred_the_n.answer: Mechanism: require lane selection from ThirdPerson/REGRESSION.md for any regression claim. Emit lane id and artifact root in a durable proof file, and refuse to claim closure without it. Additionally: detect STOP/PAUSE keywords as state transitions that disable tools and switch into 'wait' mode, and detect question prompts ('Agree/disagree', '?' forms) to force a direct answer-first response.
